_note: Unified treatment of grounding across NEC. Primary source is Article 250; references from 300, 310, 408, 450. Use this file when looking up grounding rules independently of a specific Article.

grounding_concepts.definition: Intentional connection of one conductor of the electrical system to the grounding electrode system at one point.
examples: Grounded conductor of 120/240 V single-phase service is bonded to GES at service via MBJ
Grounded conductor of 480Y/277 V wye service grounded similarly

grounding_concepts.definition: Conductive paths providing low-impedance return for ground-fault current. Bonds all normally non-current-carrying metal parts together and back to the grounded conductor at the source.
examples: Cable assembly's EGC conductor (e.g. NM cable green/bare)

grounding_concepts.definition: All grounding electrodes present at the building, bonded together to form a single grounding reference.
required_electrodes_when_present: Metal underground water pipe (≥10 ft, in direct contact with earth)
Concrete-encased electrode (Ufer) — ≥20 ft of bare/zinc-galvanized rebar, ≥1/2 in dia, in concrete in direct contact with earth
Ground ring — bare copper conductor ≥2 AWG, ≥20 ft, ≥30 in below grade
Rod/Pipe/Plate electrode (250.52(A)(5)) — typical 5/8 in dia × 8 ft Cu-clad steel rod, ≥8 ft in earth
Structural metal frame — if present and qualifying

grounding_concepts.definition: Connection between grounded service conductor (neutral) and equipment grounding terminal at the service equipment.
grounding_concepts.sizing: Per 250.28(D)(1): ≥ size of GEC required by Table 250.66, OR per 250.28(D)(2) if largest ungrounded conductor > 1100 kcmil (12.5% rule)

grounding_concepts.definition: Connection between the grounded conductor and equipment grounding system on the supply side of the service disconnect.
grounding_concepts._note: Functionally identical to MBJ but applied to SDS (transformer secondary, generator output, etc.)

grounding_concepts.definition: Conductor that provides the low-impedance fault current path back to the source. Required on every circuit.
grounding_concepts.sizing: Per Table 250.122 (by OCPD rating) OR adiabatic (250.122(B)) for special conditions (e.g. parallel conductors)
grounding_concepts.vs_iec_cpc: The NEC EGC is the analogue of IEC CPC. Sizing differs: NEC sizes by OCPD rating; IEC sizes by line conductor CSA (Table 54.1) or adiabatic.

grounding_concepts.definition: Conductor that connects the grounded service conductor (or system source) to the grounding electrode system.
grounding_concepts.sizing: Per Table 250.66 (by largest ungrounded service conductor)
grounding_concepts.vs_iec: No exact IEC analogue — IEC sizes 'main earthing conductor' adiabatically or per local supplement.

egc_sizing_matrix_summary._rule_of_thumb: EGC at least the next size up from the OCPD rating in conductor terms. For copper EGC, rough rule: OCPD ≤ 60A → 10 AWG; 60-200A → larger copper EGC needed.
egc_sizing_matrix_summary._adiabatic_when: Use 250.122(B) adiabatic check when fault current × clearing time > what Table 250.122 EGC can safely carry. Common on parallel circuits with high SCCR.

common_grounding_errors: Confusing GEC (sized by ungrounded conductor) with EGC (sized by OCPD)
Bonding neutral to ground at sub-panel — illegal except at service or first SDS disconnect (250.142)
Omitting Ufer when present — 250.50(A)(3) mandates bonding all present electrodes
Forgetting MBJ at service — without it, the grounded conductor is just a neutral, not a grounded one
Treating the metal raceway as EGC on circuits where 250.118(5) FMC length/size limits apply — must run separate wire EGC

concept_mapping.iec: Earthing arrangement classification (IEC 60364-1 Clause 312 — TN-S, TN-C, TN-C-S, TT, IT)
Main earthing conductor / protective bonding conductor (IEC 60364-5-54 Clause 543)
